summary: Verified Firebase fix for iOS Safari blank page issue. firebase.ts correctly uses lazy initialization with isSupported() check instead of module-level getMessaging(). ErrorBoundary.tsx exists and wraps Suspense in App.tsx. All public pages (/, /become-washer, /new-order) load without errors. Space switch toggle correctly hidden for unauthenticated users.
passed_tests: firebase.ts getMessagingInstance() calls isSupported() before getMessaging() (line 27-29)
Landing page (/) loads without ErrorBoundary showing
space-switch-btn not in DOM for unauthenticated users (correct behavior)
mobile-space-switch not in DOM for unauthenticated users (correct behavior)
No avatar_url in any Supabase .select() queries
No orders.city in any Supabase .select() queries on orders table

details.isSupported_import: Line 2: import { getMessaging, getToken, onMessage, isSupported } from 'firebase/messaging'
details.getMessagingInstance_async: Line 24: async function getMessagingInstance(): Promise<Messaging | null>
details.lazy_getMessaging: Line 29: messaging = getMessaging(app) - only called after isSupported() returns true
details.notification_window_check: Line 41: if (!('Notification' in window)) return null

details.class_component: Line 14: export class ErrorBoundary extends React.Component<Props, State>
details.reload_button: Lines 40-49: button with onClick={() => window.location.reload()} and text 'Recharger la page'

washer_dashboard_import.details: Line 4: import { requestNotificationPermission } from '../lib/firebase' - no breaking changes

no_invalid_columns.orders_city: NOT in any Supabase .select() queries on orders table

critical_code_review_comments: GOOD: firebase.ts correctly implements lazy initialization pattern - getMessaging() is only called inside getMessagingInstance() after isSupported() check
GOOD: This prevents the crash on iOS Safari where FCM is not supported
GOOD: ErrorBoundary provides fallback UI with reload button instead of blank page
GOOD: ErrorBoundary wraps the entire Suspense block in App.tsx, catching any component crash
GOOD: WasherDashboard import unchanged - requestNotificationPermission still works
NOTE: pushNotifications.tsx also has getMessaging() but it's wrapped in initFirebase() which checks 'serviceWorker' in navigator && 'PushManager' in window first - different but also safe approach

success_rate.frontend: 100% - All code verifications passed, all pages load correctly

test_credentials: Admin: akim.hachili@gmail.com, Client: sousouait59600@gmail.com (passwords managed by Supabase Auth)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 26 verified the critical Firebase fix for iOS Safari blank page issue. The root cause was firebase.ts calling getMessaging() at module load time, which crashes on iOS Safari (FCM not supported). Fix: lazy init with isSupported() check + ErrorBoundary as safety net. All public pages load correctly. Cannot test authenticated flows on preview (Supabase API key invalid). To test WasherDashboard with Firebase notifications, authenticate on production kilolab.fr.
screenshots_taken: .screenshots/iteration26_mobile_menu.png - Mobile menu, space switch correctly hidden for unauthenticated users
